common.loginTimeout: Login timed out, please try again

project.projectInstructionsHint: These instructions are automatically added to the system prompt of every session in this project.
project.projectInstructionsPlaceholder: e.g. Always reply in English; keep the tone clear and friendly; start with the conclusion
project.projectLogoHint: Optional. When set, replaces the emoji icon in the sidebar. Images are auto-resized to 256×256.
project.noSessionsHint: Click to create the first session
project.bindChannelHelp: Once bound, conversations from this IM chat will automatically join this project.

files.uploadHint: Drag files here or click to upload
files.noFiles: No files yet — upload one to get started

deleteConfirm.title: Delete Project
deleteConfirm.body: Sessions in this project will become unassigned (not deleted). Project memories and files will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.

workingDir.label: Working Directory
workingDir.hint: Sessions in this project default to this directory; session-level setting overrides it.

chat.howCanIHelp: Code, search, create, automate — just say the word
chat.askAnything: Tell me your idea, I'll handle the rest...
chat.pendingQueued: Message queued, will send after reply...
chat.incognitoEmptyBody: This chat won't appear in history or use/update memory, awareness, or stats. When you leave it, the local copy is deleted.
chat.incognitoAwarenessDisabled: Behavior awareness is unavailable while incognito is on
chat.incognitoMutuallyExclusive: Incognito is unavailable for this session
chat.incognitoProjectExclusive: Project sessions can't be incognito — Project memory and files are persistent by design
chat.incognitoChannelExclusive: IM Channel sessions can't be incognito — the counterparty can keep messaging asynchronously

workingDir.selectPreset: Working directory is set when you start the chat
workingDir.browserTitle: Select a directory on the server
workingDir.browserDescription: Paths are resolved on the server machine where the agent runs.
workingDir.truncated: Only the first entries are shown — this directory is larger than the browser can display.

chat.deleteSessionWarning: This will permanently delete this conversation and all its messages. This action cannot be undone. Are you sure?
chat.thinkingAutoDisabled: {{provider}} / {{model}} does not support the current thinking mode. Thinking was disabled automatically and the request was retried.
chat.toolPermissionAutoDesc: Model decides whether approval is needed

codexAuth.expiredDescription: Your Codex OAuth token is no longer valid. Please sign in again to continue.

provider.title: Providers
provider.selectProvider: Select a provider to get started
provider.codexSecurityWarning: For your account security, Claude Code authentication mode is not available at this time
provider.connectRemoteServerDesc: Use a deployed hope-agent server instead of configuring providers locally.
provider.remoteApiKeyPlaceholder: Leave empty if the server has no auth
provider.name: Name
provider.leaveEmptyNoAuth: Leave empty, fill in if authentication is needed
provider.apiKeyLeaveEmpty: API Key (leave empty to keep unchanged)
provider.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this provider?
provider.authRequired: Fill in if authentication is needed
provider.allowPrivateNetworkDesc: The Base URL points to a local / private network. Turning this on will add the host to the SSRF trusted hosts allowlist on save so LLM calls to this provider stay unblocked.

authProfiles.title: API Key Rotation Profiles
authProfiles.empty: No rotation profiles configured. The main API Key above will be used.

model.atLeastOneModel: Add at least one model to continue

settings.title: Settings
settings.modelConfigDesc: Manage model providers and global model settings
settings.toolsDesc: Configure web search, web fetch and other tools

mcp.title: MCP Servers
mcp.subtitle: Connect Model Context Protocol servers to extend the available tools.
mcp.authRequired: "{{name}}" needs authorization — your browser has been opened
mcp.emptyDesc: Add an MCP server or import one from a Claude Desktop config. stdio servers work today; remote HTTP/SSE/WebSocket transports arrive in a later phase.
mcp.confirmDelete: Delete server "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
mcp.nameImmutable: Name cannot be changed after creation. Delete and re-add to rename.
mcp.invalidName: Name must match ^[a-z0-9_-]{1,32}$ (lowercase letters, digits, underscore, hyphen)
mcp.remoteTransportPhase4: HTTP / SSE / WebSocket transports will land in Phase 4.
mcp.argsHint: One argument per line. Values support ${ENV} placeholders.
mcp.envHint: Only these vars plus a minimal allowlist are forwarded to the subprocess; values support ${VAR} expansion.
mcp.allowedToolsHint: Empty = allow all. One original tool name per line (without the mcp__ prefix).

import.title: Import MCP server config
import.description: Paste a Claude Desktop claude_desktop_config.json (or any JSON with the same shape). Each entry becomes a separate server.
import.hint: Each mcpServers key is lowercased to form the server name. Duplicates are skipped.

settings.dangerousIntro: One-click switch to bypass every tool approval. Extreme risk — enable only on fully trusted local machines.
settings.dangerousSwitchDesc: When on, every tool call runs without prompting. Overrides all global, session, and channel auto-approve settings.
settings.dangerousCliLocked: Active via CLI flag --dangerously-skip-all-approvals; this toggle is read-only for the current launch. Restart without the flag to disable.
settings.dangerousConfirmBody: The following actions will run without any approval prompt:
settings.dangerousConfirmNote: Plan Mode's tool-type restrictions still apply. You can turn this switch off here at any time.
settings.dangerousConfirmAck: I understand the risks and consent to skipping every tool approval
settings.skillsDesc: Skills provide domain expertise for the AI assistant and are automatically injected into the system prompt. Place skill folders in:

skillsImport.dialogDescription: Probe known third-party skill catalogs (Claude Code, OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, Anthropic marketplace) and add the ones you have installed.
skillsImport.notFound: No matching paths found on this machine.

warning.openclaw: OpenClaw skills often depend on external CLIs (gh, 1password, tmux); calls may fail until you install them.
warning.proprietary: Anthropic skills are proprietary — use is governed by your Anthropic Consumer/Commercial Terms.

tooltip.distinguishOnboarding: To migrate legacy OpenClaw user data, use the dedicated 'Import OpenClaw' step (step 2).

error.discover: Failed to discover preset skill sources

settings.noSkillsHint: Create a skill folder with a SKILL.md in ~/.hope-agent/skills/, or import another directory
settings.skillsDraftsDesc: Your Agent identified these reusable patterns after recent conversations and created draft skills. Activate to make them available in the system prompt.
settings.skillEnvVarsDesc: Configure the API keys and tokens required by this skill
settings.memoryDesc: Persistent memories that help the AI remember context across conversations
settings.activeMemoryDesc: At the start of each user turn, run a bounded side query to distill the single most relevant memory into one sentence and inject it alongside the system prompt. Timeouts and failures silently fall back to passive recall — they never block the chat.

memoryBudget.title: Memory Budget
memoryBudget.desc: Caps the total character count of the Memory section (Global memory.md / Agent memory.md / SQLite memories / Guidelines) and the per-layer limits inside it. Lowering the total frees budget for the live conversation context.
memoryBudget.agentOverrideDesc: Inherits the global Memory Budget by default. Toggle off to use an agent-specific budget.
memoryBudget.totalCharsDesc: Hard upper bound for the entire Memory section. Guidelines are reserved first; remaining layers drop in priority order. Default 10000.
memoryBudget.coreMemoryFileCharsDesc: Independent cap for each memory.md file (Global / Agent). Content beyond the cap is truncated in the prompt but preserved on disk. Default 8000.
memoryBudget.sqliteEntryMaxCharsDesc: Upper bound on the first line of each rendered SQLite memory bullet, preventing one long entry from crowding out same-section peers. Default 500.

settings.coreMemoryGlobalDesc: Rules and preferences shared across all agents, always visible in conversations
settings.coreMemoryAgentDesc: Rules and preferences specific to this agent, always visible in conversations
settings.openclawMemoryHint: This is equivalent to OpenClaw's MEMORY.md — same format, fully interoperable.
settings.coreMemoryPlaceholder:
Write rules and preferences you want the AI to always remember...
e.g.: Reply in English, no code comments, use pnpm only
settings.memoryFlushDesc: Auto-extract important info before context compression
settings.memoryEmpty: No memories yet. Add your first memory to help the AI remember important context.
settings.memoryEmbeddingDesc: Configure embedding models for semantic memory search. Without this, only keyword search is available.
settings.memoryEmbeddingEnabledDesc: Use embedding models to find semantically similar memories
settings.memoryEmbeddingProviderDesc: Choose one explicit embedding provider. After changing provider, model, or dimensions, re-embed existing memories.
settings.memoryEmbeddingLegacyProvider: This is still using the old {{provider}} configuration. Choose an explicit provider and save it to avoid vector-space changes from automatic switching.
settings.memoryEmbeddingReembedWarning: Embedding configuration changed. After saving, re-embed all memories so old vectors are rebuilt with the selected model.
settings.memoryDuplicateFound: Similar memories found — do you want to add anyway or update an existing one?
settings.memoryImportFromAIDesc: Ask another AI assistant to summarize what it knows about you, then paste the result here.
settings.memoryImportFromAIStep1: Step 1 — Copy this prompt and send it to another AI
settings.memoryImportFromAIStep2: Step 2 — Paste the AI's response below
settings.memoryImportFromAIPastePlaceholder: Paste the JSON array returned by the other AI here...
settings.memoryImportFromAIEmpty: Please paste the AI response first.
settings.memoryAutoExtractDesc: Automatically extract memories when token, time, or message thresholds are met
settings.memoryDedupAdvancedDesc: Adjust RRF similarity thresholds for memory deduplication. Higher values make it easier to detect duplicates.
settings.memoryEmbeddingAutoDesc: Automatically selects the best available embedding provider (local model first, then reuses configured LLM API keys). No extra setup needed.
settings.memorySearchTuningDesc: Adjust hybrid search weights, temporal decay, and diversity reranking parameters.
settings.memoryTemporalDecayDesc: When enabled, older memories gradually rank lower in search (pinned memories are exempt).
settings.memoryMmrDesc: Uses MMR algorithm to avoid redundant results and improve diversity in search output.
settings.memoryEmbeddingCacheDesc: Caches computed vectors to avoid redundant API calls for repeated text, saving cost.
settings.memoryMultimodalDesc: Enable image/audio file embedding. Only supported with Google Gemini gemini-embedding-2 model.
settings.memoryMultimodalRequiresGemini: Multimodal embedding requires Google Gemini with gemini-embedding-2 model
settings.sandboxDesc: Configure Docker container sandbox isolation for secure command execution

browser.title: Browser
browser.desc: Hope Agent can launch and drive a dedicated Chrome instance for you. Create separate profiles to keep logins (Google, GitHub, work tools…) isolated — the agent and you share the same session, so whatever you sign into, the agent can operate with.
browser.launchHelp: Pick a profile below, then click Launch. A Chrome window will open — log in once and your cookies are kept in the profile directory for next time.
browser.profileHint: Profile '{{name}}' — cookies and logins will persist.
browser.profileNoneHint: Without a profile, Chrome runs with a throwaway session. Create a profile to keep logins.
browser.executableHint: Leave empty to auto-detect. Only set if you want a specific Chrome/Chromium build.
browser.headlessHint: Hide the browser window. Off by default so you can sign in.
browser.profilesHelp: Each profile is a separate Chrome user-data-dir. Create one per workflow (e.g. personal-google, work-github) and sign in ahead of time — the agent uses whichever profile you launched with.
browser.profilesEmpty: No profiles yet. Create one above to save logins between sessions.
browser.deleteDisabledActive: Stop the browser before deleting its profile
browser.deleteConfirmDesc: All cookies, logins and cached data for this profile will be removed. This cannot be undone.
browser.connectHelp: Already running Chrome with --remote-debugging-port=9222? Attach instead of launching a new instance.

settings.channelsDesc: Manage Telegram, WeChat, Feishu, Discord, Slack and other IM channel integrations
settings.acpControlDesc: Manage external ACP agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, etc.)
settings.acpEnabledDesc: Allow agents to spawn external ACP agents for task delegation
settings.acpAutoDiscoverDesc: Scan $PATH for known ACP agent binaries on startup
settings.sandboxDockerNotInstalled: Docker not detected, please install Docker Desktop first
settings.sandboxDockerNotRunning: Docker is installed but not running, please start Docker Desktop
settings.sandboxImageDesc: Docker image used for sandbox containers
settings.sandboxReadOnlyDesc: Mount root filesystem as read-only, only /workspace and /tmp are writable
settings.sandboxCapDropDesc: Remove all Linux capabilities to prevent privilege escalation
settings.sandboxNoNewPrivilegesDesc: Prevent child processes from gaining additional privileges
settings.sandboxNetworkModeDesc: Network access mode for the container
settings.webSearchDesc: Drag to reorder search providers. The first enabled provider will be used. Providers requiring an API key must have credentials configured before enabling.
settings.webSearchDockerNotRunning: Docker is installed but not running. Please start Docker first.
settings.webSearchDockerUseProxyDesc: When enabled, the current app/system proxy is written into Docker SearXNG. If your host VPN already handles routing, try turning this off and restarting or redeploying the container.
settings.webSearchDockerSaveConfigFailed: Failed to save SearXNG Docker settings. Save the settings and try again.
settings.webSearchDockerStepPullingImage: Pulling SearXNG image (may take a few minutes)…
settings.webSearchDockerStepHealthCheck: Waiting for SearXNG to be ready…
settings.webSearchDockerSearchFail: SearXNG connected but search returned no results
settings.webSearchCacheTtlDesc: Cache results for this duration (0 = disabled)
settings.webFetchDesc: Configure how the web_fetch tool extracts content from URLs, including caching, security, and content limits.
settings.webFetchMaxCharsDesc: Default maximum characters returned to the model
settings.webFetchMaxCharsCapDesc: Hard limit on max_chars parameter from tool calls
settings.webFetchMaxResponseBytesDesc: Maximum HTTP response body size to download
settings.webFetchCacheTtlDesc: How long to cache fetched pages. Set to 0 to disable caching.
settings.webFetchSsrfDesc: Block requests to private/internal network addresses
settings.imageGenerateDesc: Configure AI image generation providers (OpenAI, Google, Fal, MiniMax, SiliconFlow, ZhipuAI, Tongyi). Once enabled, Agent can generate and edit images via the image_generate tool.
settings.imageGenNoProvider: No provider configured. Enable one and enter an API Key to let Agent generate images.
settings.imageGenPriorityHint: Providers are tried in priority order with automatic failover
settings.canvasDesc: Interactive canvas tool: AI can create and manage HTML/Markdown/charts/slides and other visual content, rendered live in a preview panel.
settings.canvasAutoShowDesc: Automatically open the preview panel when canvas is created or updated
settings.canvasClearAllConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete all canvas projects? This cannot be undone.
settings.permDesc: Hope Agent needs the following system permissions to control your computer. Please grant them in System Settings.
settings.permAccessibilityDesc: Control mouse, keyboard and interact with other application windows
settings.permScreenRecordingDesc: Capture screen content and recognize UI elements
settings.permAutomationDesc: Control other applications via AppleScript (e.g. Finder, System Events)
settings.permAppManagementDesc: Update or delete other applications' data
settings.permFullDiskAccessDesc: Read and write protected files and directories
settings.permFilesAndFoldersDesc: Access Desktop, Documents, Downloads and other user directories
settings.permCameraDesc: Use the camera for visual recognition
settings.permLocalNetworkDesc: Discover and connect to devices on the local network
settings.permGrantTooltip: Opens System Settings to grant permission
settings.permRefreshHint: After granting in System Settings, click refresh or switch back to auto-detect
settings.ssrfDesc: Restrict outbound requests from browser, web fetch, image generation, and link preview tools from reaching private networks and cloud metadata endpoints to defend against SSRF attacks.
settings.ssrfDefaultPolicyDesc: Tools without an explicit override inherit this policy.
settings.ssrfBrowserPolicyDesc: Applied to page.goto / new_page target URL. Note: JS fetch() inside browser.evaluate is out of scope.
settings.ssrfWebFetchPolicyDesc: Applied to URLs fetched by web_fetch.
settings.ssrfImageGeneratePolicyDesc: Applied when image_generate loads a reference image via URL.
settings.ssrfUrlPreviewPolicyDesc: Applied to link preview fetches in chat messages.
settings.ssrfPolicyAllowPrivate: Lenient (allow private, still block cloud metadata)
settings.ssrfTrustedHostsDesc: Hosts in this list bypass SSRF policy checks. Supports host, host:port, and *.example.com wildcard. Use to explicitly allow self-hosted Ollama / LM Studio etc.
settings.recapDefaultRangeDaysDesc: Time window used when no previous report exists. Default 30.
settings.recapMaxSessionsDesc: Hard cap on how many sessions a single report analyzes. Default 500.
settings.recapFacetConcurrencyDesc: Parallel side-query calls when extracting per-session facets. Lower if you hit rate limits. Default 4.
settings.recapCacheRetentionDaysDesc: How long cached session facets are kept before a daily sweep deletes them. Set to 0 to keep forever. Default 180.
settings.recapAnalysisAgentDesc: Agent used to extract facets and write report sections. Falls back to the active agent when unset.
settings.chatAutoSendDesc: Automatically send queued messages after reply finishes. When off, message is filled back to input.
settings.chatAutoExpandThinkingDesc: Automatically expand thinking blocks in chat bubbles. When off, click to expand manually.
settings.toolCallNarrationDesc: When enabled, instruct the model to preface every tool call with a one-sentence announcement (Claude Code style). Some models (e.g. GPT-5.4) over-apply this and restate the same intent across consecutive tool calls, creating visible duplication. Default off.
settings.sessionTitleDesc: Keep the first-message title immediately, then generate a concise title after the first assistant reply.
settings.sessionTitleChatModelHint: Uses the main chat model for the one-time title generation.
settings.sessionTitleCustomModelHint: Uses the selected provider/model for title generation.

awareness.title: Behavior Awareness
awareness.desc: Give this chat a dynamic view of what the user is doing in other parallel sessions.
awareness.llmWarning: LLM Digest mode runs an extra side_query per user turn (throttled to min_interval_secs). Expect extra API cost.
awareness.perSessionHint: Per-session overrides are also available via the /awareness command or API.

settings.planSubagentDesc: When enabled, Plan Mode uses a dedicated sub-agent for plan creation (cleaner main context). When disabled, planning runs inline in the main agent (context continuity).
settings.planQuestionTimeoutDesc: Maximum time to wait for user response to Plan Mode questions. Auto-cancels the question on timeout. Set to 0 for no limit.
settings.contextCompactDesc: Automatically compress conversation context to prevent exceeding model limits
settings.contextCompactEnabledDesc: Automatically trim old tool results and summarize old messages when conversation gets long
settings.contextCompactMicrocompactEnabledDesc: Zero-cost clearing of old ephemeral tool results (ls/grep/find etc.), runs before other compression tiers
settings.contextCompactMicrocompactToolsDesc: Old results from selected tools will be automatically cleared
settings.contextCompactToolPolicyDesc: Control how each tool's results are handled during context compression
settings.contextCompactMaxToolResultShareDesc: Max share of context window a single tool result can occupy (10%–60%). Increase to preserve larger file reads and web fetches
settings.contextCompactSoftTrimRatioDesc: Start head+tail trimming of old tool results when context usage exceeds this ratio
settings.contextCompactHardClearRatioDesc: Replace old tool results with placeholders when still exceeding this ratio after soft trim
settings.contextCompactKeepAssistantsDesc: Tool results from the last N assistant replies are never pruned
settings.contextCompactToolsDenyDesc: Results from these tools are always kept in full, comma separated
settings.contextCompactSummarizationModelCacheHin: Uses prompt cache sharing with conversation model, ~90% cache hit rate for minimal token cost
settings.contextCompactSummarizationModelCustomHi: Uses dedicated model for summarization. On failure, automatically falls back to conversation model with cache sharing
settings.contextCompactSummarizationThresholdDesc: Trigger LLM summarization of old messages when still exceeding this ratio after pruning
settings.contextCompactPreserveTurnsDesc: Keep the last N conversation turns verbatim during summarization
settings.contextCompactIdentifierInstructionsPlac: e.g.: Preserve all file paths and URLs exactly, other IDs may be abbreviated
settings.contextCompactMaxSummaryCharsDesc: Maximum characters for compaction summary (4000–64000). Increase for richer context retention, but the summary itself takes more space
settings.contextCompactSoftTrimMaxChars: Min result size for soft trim
settings.contextCompactSoftTrimMaxCharsDesc: Tool results smaller than this are skipped during soft trim
settings.contextCompactCacheTtlDesc: Skip pruning/summarization if last compaction was within this time. Preserves API prompt cache. 0 = disabled
settings.contextCompactReactiveMicrocompactDesc: Between tool loop rounds, when usage crosses the threshold, clear ephemeral tool_results (ls/grep/find/…). Tier 0 — zero cost, keeps prompt cache valid.
settings.contextCompactReactiveTriggerRatioDesc: Usage ratio that triggers reactive microcompaction (default 75%).
settings.contextCompactMinPrunableCharsDesc: Skip hard clear if total prunable chars below this value
settings.systemAutostartDesc: Automatically start Hope Agent when you log in to your computer
settings.defaultAgentDesc: Used when no agent is specified, falling back through Project → IM channel → this default.
settings.configBackupsDesc: Every settings change is snapshotted automatically. Roll back to any previous version (last 50 kept).
settings.configBackupsRestoreDesc: Will restore config to {{timestamp}} ({{kind}} / {{category}}). The current state is snapshotted first, so you can undo the rollback.
settings.proxySettingsDesc: Configure proxy for all HTTP requests
settings.proxyModeCustomDesc: Use a specific proxy server address
settings.toolTimeoutDesc: Max execution time per tool call. Prevents infinite hangs from network issues. Set to 0 to disable
settings.approvalTimeoutDesc: Max time to wait for a tool approval response. Set to 0 to disable
settings.approvalTimeoutActionDesc: What to do when approval wait times out
settings.asyncToolsDesc: Long-running tools (exec, web_search, image_generate) can be transferred to background jobs so the conversation can continue. Results are auto-injected when ready.
settings.asyncToolsEnabledDesc: Master switch. When off, run_in_background is ignored and every tool runs synchronously.
settings.asyncToolsAutoBackgroundDesc: Sync calls exceeding this duration are automatically transferred to a background job. 0 = disable auto-transfer.
settings.asyncToolsMaxJobDesc: Hard limit on how long a background job may run before being killed. 0 = no per-job limit (still bounded by tool timeout).
settings.asyncToolsJobStatusMaxWaitDesc: Upper bound for a single job_status(block=true) call. Only applied when max job duration = 0; otherwise max job duration takes precedence.
settings.asyncToolsRetentionDesc: Completed job rows and their spool files are purged after this many days. 0 = keep forever.
settings.asyncToolsOrphanGraceDesc: Spool files not referenced by any DB row are cleaned up after this many hours. Grace window prevents races with in-flight writes.
settings.asyncToolsInlineBytesDesc: Result bytes inlined in the completion notification. Larger results are spooled with a head/tail preview.
settings.deferredToolsEnabledDesc: Enable tool_search-based loading. Tools remain injected until you defer them below. Takes effect on the next conversation.
settings.deferredToolsPerToolDesc: Turn on only the tools whose schemas should be discovered via tool_search instead of sent up front.
settings.memorySelectionDesc: When the candidate memory count exceeds the threshold, ask the model to pick the most relevant ones for the system prompt. Reduces noise.
settings.memorySelectionThresholdDesc: Kick in only when this many candidates are retrieved. Default 8.
settings.memorySelectionMaxSelectedDesc: Upper bound on memories injected into the prompt. Default 5.
settings.toolResultDiskThresholdDesc: Results exceeding this size are saved to disk, with only a preview kept in context. Set to 0 to disable
settings.maxImagesDesc: Max images allowed in a single image tool call (cap: 20)
settings.maxPdfsDesc: Max PDFs allowed in a single pdf tool call (cap: 10)
settings.maxVisionPagesDesc: Max pages rendered as images per PDF in vision mode
settings.profileDesc: Set your personal info to help the AI assistant know you better. All fields are optional — it's entirely up to you what to share for your privacy.
settings.profileLanguageHint: The model will reply in this language.
settings.profileAiExpExpert: Expert — deep user of AI tools
settings.profileCustomInfoPlaceholder: Anything else you'd like the AI to know...
settings.weatherSectionDesc: Set your location to help AI understand your local weather
settings.weatherEnabledDesc: When enabled, AI will know your current weather conditions. Powered by Open-Meteo
settings.weatherFetchError: Failed to fetch weather or pending refresh
settings.weatherLocationFailed: Location detection failed, please search manually
settings.teamsDesc: Manage preset team templates. Each member can be bound to a specific Agent, model, and default task, so the model can pick a matching preset at runtime instead of improvising.
settings.teamsEmptyDesc: Create a preset so the model can instantiate a ready-made team with specialized members instead of composing one on the fly.
settings.teamTemplateDescHint: When to pick this preset (shown to the model via list_templates)
settings.teamMemberDescriptionHint: Injected into this member's system prompt so they act in-character from turn 1.
settings.teamDeleteConfirmDesc: "{{name}}" will be removed. Existing teams created from this template keep running — only future list_templates calls are affected.
settings.teamValidationMembers: At least one member is required
settings.agentsDesc: Define and manage AI Agents. Each Agent can have a different identity, capabilities, and behavior.
settings.agentNewIdHint: Used as directory name, only letters, numbers, and hyphens
settings.agentCapabilitiesToolInjectionDesc: Choose which built-in tools this agent can use (internal system tools are always available)
settings.agentTierCoreDesc: Foundational capabilities every agent needs. Cannot be disabled.
settings.agentTierStandardDesc: Available by default. You can turn individual tools off.
settings.agentTierConfiguredDesc: Even when enabled here, a global provider must be configured before they actually work.
settings.agentMcpDesc: When enabled, all connected MCP servers and their tools become available; when off, every MCP tool is excluded.
settings.agentSkillsDesc: Globally enabled skills are available by default. You can disable specific ones for this Agent.
settings.agentRequireApprovalDesc: Control whether AI needs your confirmation before using tools
settings.toolExecDesc: Run shell commands with background and sandbox support
settings.toolProcessDesc: Manage background processes: list, poll, log, write, kill
settings.toolReadDesc: Read file contents with pagination and image detection
settings.toolWriteDesc: Write content to a file, auto-creates parent directories
settings.toolEditDesc: Search-replace edits, more precise than full write
settings.toolLsDesc: List files and directories in a path
settings.toolGrepDesc: Search file contents with regex or literal patterns
settings.toolApplyPatchDesc: Batch create, modify, delete, or move files
settings.toolWebSearchDesc: Search the web for real-time information
settings.toolWebFetchDesc: Fetch and extract content from web pages
settings.toolUpdateMemoryDesc: Update the content of an existing memory
settings.toolManageCronDesc: Create, modify, and delete scheduled tasks
settings.toolGetWeatherDesc: Fetch real-time weather data for a specified city
settings.toolSubagentDesc: Delegate tasks to subagent for execution
settings.toolTaskCreateDesc: Create a trackable task for the current session
settings.toolTaskUpdateDesc: Update the status or content of an existing task
settings.toolTaskListDesc: List all tasks in the current session
settings.toolMcpResourceDesc: Read resources hosted by connected MCP servers (files, records, etc.)
settings.toolMcpPromptDesc: Fetch prompt templates published by connected MCP servers
settings.agentSkillEnvCheckDesc: Before injecting a skill, verify its required binaries, env vars, and OS are available; skip if not met
settings.agentMdDesc: Describe this Agent's responsibilities, workflow, and principles
settings.agentMdPlaceholder: Write core instructions for this Agent...
settings.agentPersonaDesc: Define the Agent's personality, tone, and communication style
settings.agentToolsGuideDesc: Customize how the Agent uses tools
settings.agentRolePlaceholder: e.g. AI coding assistant, creative writer, research analyst...
settings.personaModeDesc: Fill in structured fields, or write a SOUL.md markdown directly. The SOUL.md file is shared with openclaw compatibility mode.
settings.personaSoulEditorDesc: Define the agent's identity, tone and principles in Markdown. Once saved, the system prompt injects this block and the structured fields are ignored (but preserved).
settings.personaSoulPlaceholder:
# Agent Name — Who You Are

## Role
...

## Tone
...
settings.agentVibeDesc: Describe the overall feel of this Agent
settings.agentVibePlaceholder: e.g. warm and patient, sharp and direct, humorous...
settings.agentTonePlaceholder: Pick a preset or type custom tone
settings.agentTraitsDesc: Define the Agent's core personality traits
settings.agentTraitsPlaceholder: Type a trait and press Enter, e.g. detail-oriented, curious...
settings.agentPrinciplesPlaceholder: Type a principle and press Enter, e.g. Always explain reasoning...
settings.agentBoundariesDesc: Define what the Agent will and won't do
settings.agentQuirksDesc: Unique habits, catchphrases, or personality details
settings.agentQuirksPlaceholder: e.g. likes to use analogies, confirms understanding before answering...
settings.agentCommStyleDesc: How the Agent organizes and presents responses
settings.agentCommStylePlaceholder: e.g. verbose with examples, minimal and terse...
settings.agentIdentitySupplementDesc: Anything else you'd like to add?
settings.agentPersonaSupplementDesc: Anything else you'd like to add?
settings.agentOpenClawModeDesc: Use OpenClaw-style 4-file configuration (AGENTS.md, IDENTITY.md, SOUL.md, TOOLS.md)
settings.agentOpenClawModeWarning: When enabled, the Identity and Personality tabs are disabled. Agent behavior is defined entirely by the 4 markdown files below.
settings.openclawMemoryMdHint: MEMORY.md content should be edited in the Memory tab.
settings.openclawModeActiveHint: OpenClaw Compatible Mode is enabled. Edit these settings in the OpenClaw tab.
settings.agentAgentsMdDesc: Workspace rules, red lines, memory guidance, and behavioral instructions
settings.agentIdentityMdDesc: Agent name, creature type, vibe, emoji, avatar — simple identity metadata
settings.agentSoulMdDesc: Agent personality, values, tone, boundaries, core truths
settings.agentToolsMdOcDesc: Local environment notes: camera names, SSH hosts, TTS voice preferences, device nicknames
settings.agentCustomPromptDesc: When enabled, custom prompts are used instead of structured settings
settings.agentCustomPromptWarning: When enabled, the settings in Identity and Personality pages will no longer take effect. The AI will follow only the custom content below.
settings.agentCustomIdentityDesc: Custom Agent identity, responsibilities, and workflow
settings.agentCustomPersonaDesc: Custom Agent personality, tone, and communication style
settings.agentDeleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this Agent?
settings.subagentAllowedAgentsHint: Select which agents can be delegated to. Unchecked agents will be blocked.
settings.agentNoAgentsHint: Create a new Agent to customize AI behavior
settings.openclawImportDesc: Scan and import agents from your OpenClaw configuration
settings.openclawImportNoAgentsHint: Make sure ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json exists and contains agents
settings.charLimitExceeded: Exceeded limit, excess content will be truncated
settings.fallbackModelsDesc: When the primary model is unavailable, try these models in order
settings.agentPlanModelDesc: Use a cheaper/faster model during the planning phase to reduce costs by 60-80%. The primary model is used during execution.
settings.globalTemperatureDesc: Controls randomness of model output. Lower values are more focused, higher values are more creative. Can be overridden per agent or per session.
settings.agentTemperatureDesc: Override global temperature for this agent. Reset to inherit from global settings.
settings.agentModelCustomDesc: Override global model settings with agent-specific model and fallback chain
settings.agentModelInheritHint: Currently inheriting global default and fallback model settings. Enable "Custom Model" to configure agent-specific models.
settings.agentModelInheritDesc: Uses the global default and fallback model settings
settings.logsDesc: View and manage detailed execution logs. Filter by level, category, and keyword search.
settings.logsClearDesc: This will permanently delete all log records. This action cannot be undone. Are you sure?
settings.logsEnabledDesc: When disabled, no new logs will be recorded
settings.logsFileEnabledDesc: Output plain text log files for external tools and Agent self-inspection
settings.logsSelectFile: Select a file on the left to view
settings.devWarningDesc: The following actions will permanently delete data and cannot be undone. The app will restart automatically after execution.
settings.devClearSessionsDesc: Delete all chat history and attachments
settings.devClearCronDesc: Delete all scheduled tasks and run logs
settings.devResetConfigDesc: Reset all system settings to defaults
settings.devClearAllDesc: Clear all data and reset to defaults
settings.devConfirmDesc: This action cannot be undone. The app will restart to reinitialize. Are you sure?
settings.serverDesc: Configure server mode and remote connection
settings.serverModeDesc: Choose embedded mode or connect to a remote server
settings.serverModeEmbeddedDesc: Desktop app runs an HTTP server on localhost
settings.serverModeRemoteDesc: Connect to an external Hope Agent server
settings.serverEmbeddedBindDesc: Default 127.0.0.1 for localhost only. Set to 0.0.0.0 to expose to the network
settings.serverRestartRequired: Restart the app for embedded server changes to take effect

localLlm.title: Local Model Assistant
localLlm.subtitle: Detect your hardware and install a Qwen3.6 / Gemma 4 model in one click

hardware.macOs: macOS · {{memory}} GB unified memory · 60% budget ≈ {{budget}} GB to avoid overuse!
hardware.dgpu: {{gpu}} · {{vram}} GB VRAM · 60% budget ≈ {{budget}} GB to avoid overuse!
hardware.ramFallback: {{memory}} GB RAM · 60% budget ≈ {{budget}} GB to avoid overuse!
hardware.insufficient: Hardware too modest — keep using a cloud provider for now.

localEmbedding.title: Local Embedding Assistant
localEmbedding.subtitle: Install a small Ollama embedding model and enable vector search
localEmbedding.reembedHint: Embedding dimensions changed. Use Re-embed All to update existing memories.
localEmbedding.confirmEnableDesc: This will install/download {{model}}, create an embedding model config, and set it as the default memory model. All memory vectors will be rebuilt after confirmation.

embeddingModels.title: Embedding Model Configs
embeddingModels.desc: Configure reusable embedding models first, then choose one as the memory vector search model.
embeddingModels.empty: No embedding model configs yet. Create one from a template above, or add a custom config.
embeddingModels.emptyMemory: No embedding model config is available yet. Create one first, or use the local embedding quick card.
embeddingModels.memoryDefaultDesc: Switching the default memory model rebuilds all memory vectors so old and new vectors are not mixed.
embeddingModels.selectToEnable: Select an embedding model as the default memory model
embeddingModels.confirmSwitchDesc: This will set {{model}} as the default memory model and rebuild all memory vectors. Vector search may be temporarily incomplete while rebuilding.
embeddingModels.reembedFailed: The default model changed, but starting the rebuild failed. Retry from the rebuild card.
embeddingModels.deleteDesc: This will delete the config for {{model}}. The current default memory model cannot be deleted directly.
embeddingModels.name: Config name

embeddingActivation.title: Choose an embedding model
embeddingActivation.description: Vector search needs an embedding model. Pick one to enable and rebuild memory vectors.
embeddingActivation.empty: No embedding model is configured yet. Add one to enable vector search.

reembedMode.label: Rebuild mode
reembedMode.keepExistingDesc: Old vectors stay searchable; the new model overwrites them entry by entry.
reembedMode.deleteAllDesc: Wipe every memory's vector before rebuilding. Search will only return keyword matches until the rebuild finishes.

reembedJob.title: Memory vector rebuild
reembedJob.idle: {{count}} memories under the active model. Trigger a manual rebuild any time.
reembedJob.idleDisabled: Enable vector search and add memories before rebuilding.

localModels.title: Local Models
localModels.subtitle: Manage Ollama models installed on this machine: search, download, preload, unload, delete, and connect them to chat or memory.
localModels.searchPlaceholder: Search Ollama models, e.g. qwen, gemma, embedding
localModels.searchHint: Search the Ollama Library to find downloadable models.
localModels.detailPlaceholder: Select a model family to view downloadable versions.
localModels.emptyInstalledDesc: Download a model from the Library tab or enter an Ollama model name manually.
localModels.cacheStale: Showing cached library results because the live search is unavailable.
localModels.largeModelWarning: Large model - may exceed {{budget}} local budget

delete.title: Delete local model?
delete.description: {{model}} will be removed from Ollama local storage. This cannot be undone.
delete.running: The model is currently loaded and will be unloaded first.
delete.active: It is the global default model; that reference will be cleared.
delete.fallback: It is in the fallback model chain; that reference will be removed.
delete.provider: It is registered in the Ollama provider; that model entry will be removed.
delete.embeddingConfig: It is added to Embedding model configs; the matching config will be removed.
delete.embedding: It is the active memory embedding model; local embedding will be disabled.

skillsEvolutionHero.title: Distill what you keep doing into reusable skills
skillsEvolutionHero.body: Hope Agent doesn't stay still. Every so often it replays your chats, turns the things you keep doing into reusable skills — so the next time it comes up, the agent already knows how.
skillsEvolutionHero.note: On by default · grows more capable the more you use it. Turn off to keep things as-is — no new skills will form on their own.

skillsAutoReview.label: Auto-activate new skills
skillsAutoReview.description: Skip the draft step — newly distilled skills go live immediately.
skillsAutoReview.hint: Off (default): new skills land as drafts and need your approval first.

channels.title: IM Channels
channels.description: Connect Telegram, WeChat, Feishu, Discord, Slack and other IM platforms to chat with AI Agent
channels.noAccounts: No IM channel accounts configured yet
channels.duplicateCredential: This bot is already added (existing account: {{label}}). Duplicates are not allowed.
channels.telegramTokenHint: Get a Bot Token from Telegram @BotFather
channels.userAllowlistPlaceholder: Type a user ID and press Enter to add
channels.userAllowlistHint: Enter allowed sender IDs and press Enter to confirm
channels.wechatConnectionHint: Use QR login to obtain the WeChat token and long-poll base URL automatically. No manual token entry is required.
channels.wechatScanHint: Scan the QR code in WeChat and confirm on your phone.
channels.wechatScannedHint: QR code scanned. Confirm login in WeChat.
channels.wechatExpiredHint: QR code expired. Generate a new one and scan again.
channels.boundAgentHint: Select which Agent this channel will chat with. Different channels can bind different Agents.
channels.defaultRequireMentionHint: Bot only responds when @mentioned or replied to in groups. Each group can override this.
channels.groupConfigHint: Set per-group reply policies and permissions. Use * as ID for fallback defaults.
channels.groupAllowFromHint: Telegram user IDs allowed in this group. Empty = no restriction.
channels.groupSystemPromptPlaceholder: Inject extra system prompt for this group...
channels.channelConfigHint: Configure bot behavior for Telegram channels (broadcasts)
channels.autoApproveToolsHint: When enabled, all tool executions via this IM channel will be automatically approved without manual confirmation
channels.discordTokenHint: Create a Bot at Discord Developer Portal and copy the token
channels.slackTokenHint: Enable Socket Mode in your Slack App settings to get the App Token
channels.feishuTokenHint: Create a Bot in Feishu Open Platform and get App ID / App Secret
channels.qqbotTokenHint: Create a Bot at QQ Open Platform (q.qq.com) and get credentials
channels.ircHint: Connect to any IRC server with optional TLS encryption
channels.signalHint: Requires signal-cli installed and registered locally. Run 'signal-cli link' or 'signal-cli register' first.
channels.imessageHint: macOS only. Requires the imsg CLI tool installed and in your PATH.
channels.whatsappHint: Requires a WhatsApp bridge service running. Point to its HTTP API URL.
channels.gchatHint: Requires a Google Workspace service account with Chat API enabled
channels.webhookUrlHint: Desktop apps need a public URL (e.g. ngrok) to receive webhooks
channels.lineHint: Get credentials from LINE Developers Console (Messaging API channel)

cron.title: Scheduled Tasks
cron.name: Task Name
cron.cronHelp: Format: sec min hour day month weekday (e.g. 0 0 9 * * 1-5 = weekdays 9am)
cron.messagePlaceholder: The instruction sent to the agent when triggered, e.g. Summarize today's progress
cron.deliveryTargetsDesc: Fan out the job's final output to the selected IM conversations (failures send a short error notice)
cron.noDeliveryTargets: No targets — results only land in session history
cron.noDeliveryChannels: No enabled IM channel accounts. Configure one under Settings → Channels first
cron.noConversationsYet: This account has no recorded conversations yet (have the bot receive a message first)
cron.deleteConfirmDesc: This will permanently remove the scheduled task and its run history.

notification.title: Notification Settings
notification.globalDesc: Receive native desktop notifications for background events
notification.agentDesc: Per-agent notification overrides. When set to Default, uses the global setting.
notification.cronNote: Cron task notifications can be configured per-job in the Scheduled Tasks settings panel.
notification.cronNotifyDesc: Send a desktop notification when this task finishes

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.desc: Customize global keyboard shortcuts. Click on a shortcut area and press a new key combination to change it.
shortcuts.actionQuickChatDesc: Toggle the quick chat overlay globally
shortcuts.hint: Shortcuts must include at least one modifier key (Cmd/Alt/Shift/Ctrl). Press Esc to cancel recording.
shortcuts.chordHint: Chord shortcuts supported: press a second combo within 1.5s to record a two-part shortcut (e.g. Ctrl+K Ctrl+C).

about.title: About
about.description: It can run on your desktop, on a local server, and across IM channels, handling everyday chat while retaining context, using tools, and staying available over time.
about.tagline: A personal AI assistant that remembers, grows, and integrates deeply with your system.
about.featureDailyTitle: Designed to be opened every day
about.featureDailyDesc: Clear UI, calm defaults, and a low-friction setup that does not assume you want to learn a stack of commands first.
about.featureMemoryDesc: Sessions, memory, skills, and projects connect so the assistant stays useful instead of restarting from zero.
about.featureReachTitle: Shows up where work actually happens
about.featureReachDesc: Use it as a local desktop app, keep it running as a service, or plug it into channels and scheduled routines.
about.closing: It is not designed as a feature checklist. It is designed to stay useful, reliable, and unobtrusive over long-term use.
about.updateReady: Check GitHub Releases for a newer desktop build or let Hope Agent install one for you.
about.updateUpToDate: You're up to date on v{{version}}.
about.updateAvailable: Version v{{version}} is ready to install.
about.updateCheckFailed: Couldn't check for updates right now.

health.refreshTooltip: Refresh crash history and backup list
health.clearConfirmDesc: Are you sure you want to clear all crash history? This action cannot be undone.
health.noCrashes: No crash records. The application is running normally.
health.restoreConfirmDesc: Are you sure you want to restore from this backup? Your current configuration will be overwritten.
health.guardianEnabledDesc: When enabled, the app will automatically restart on abnormal exits, and perform backup and AI-powered diagnosis on consecutive crashes. Takes effect on next launch.
health.recoveryBannerDesc: The app crashed {{count}} time(s) before this launch and was automatically restarted.

openrouter.name: OpenRouter
openrouter.description: Multi-model aggregation gateway, hundreds of models with one key

planMode.restricted: Plan Mode: AI will gather info and collaborate with you to create a plan
planMode.executionIdle: Plan execution is waiting to continue
planMode.placeholder: Describe what you want to achieve...
planMode.editorPlaceholder:
Edit plan content here...

## Context
Describe the context: what problem to solve, why, and expected outcome

## Steps
1. First major step
   - Detail
2. Second major step
   - Detail

## Verification
How to verify the result
planMode.executeCommand: Start executing the plan. Create todos from the approved plan, execute them in order, and call update_plan_step to update high-level plan progress.

comment.hint: Select text to add comments and collaboratively refine the plan
comment.placeholder: Suggest changes for the selected content...

context.title: Context window
context.estimateNote: Estimated (char÷4); may differ from billed usage by 10–20%.

recap.noReports: No reports yet. Click Generate to build your first recap.
recap.selectReport: Select a report from the history dropdown.

learning.title: Learning Tracker
learning.subtitle: Track auto-created skills, reflective memories, and cross-session recall effectiveness
learning.noRecall: No recalls triggered in this window

dreaming.title: Dream Diary
dreaming.subtitle: Offline consolidation of recent memories — the agent reviews what's worth pinning into core memory.
dreaming.empty: No diaries yet — click Run now to generate the first one.
dreaming.selectDiary: Pick a diary on the left to read the narrative.

system.cpuDesc: Hope Agent uses {{percent}}% CPU ({{cores}} cores total)
system.liveHistoryHint: Enable auto-refresh to record CPU / memory samples over time

team.confirmDissolve: Dissolve this team? All members will be terminated.
team.sendMessage: Send a message to the team...

dangerousMode.bannerBoth: Dangerous Mode active (CLI + config) — all tool approvals bypassed
dangerousMode.bannerCli: Dangerous Mode active (started via CLI flag) — relaunch without --dangerously-skip-all-approvals to disable
dangerousMode.bannerConfig: Dangerous Mode active — all tool approvals bypassed. Turn off in Settings → Security

localModelJobs.title: Local Model Management
localModelJobs.subtitle: Local model tasks keep running in the background.
localModelJobs.emptyDescription: Ollama installs, model downloads, and model starts will appear here.

close.title: Close install window?
close.description: This task can continue in the background, or you can cancel it now.
close.taskDescription: This task can continue in the background, or you can cancel it now.

cancelConfirm.title: Cancel local model task?
cancelConfirm.description: This will stop {{model}} and delete this task record.

errors.loadFailed: Failed to load model install tasks

welcome.title: Welcome to Hope Agent
welcome.subtitle:
An AI assistant that knows you better the more you use it — on desktop, in the cloud, across IM, always on call.
A quick walkthrough follows — every step is optional except picking a model.

importOpenClaw.headline: Import from OpenClaw
importOpenClaw.description:
Already have OpenClaw configured? We can pull in your providers, API keys, agents, and notebook memories in one shot.
Safe to skip if you don't use OpenClaw.
importOpenClaw.title: What we found
importOpenClaw.subtitle: Detected at:
importOpenClaw.notFound: No OpenClaw configuration detected. You can continue with a fresh setup.
importOpenClaw.empty: OpenClaw directory found but it's empty — nothing to import.

provider.title: Pick a model provider
provider.subtitle: Connect an existing API key (OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek…) or run a local model. You can change or add more later in Settings.

profile.title: Tell us a little about yourself
profile.subtitle: All fields are optional. This personalizes how the AI addresses and responds to you.
profile.name: Display name

experience.label: How experienced are you with AI assistants?

personality.title: Pick a personality
personality.subtitle: Choose a starting vibe for the default agent. You can fine-tune or change it later in Settings → Agents.

default.name: Default assistant
default.desc: Balanced, neutral, clear and concise — a reliable general-purpose helper.

engineer.name: Professional engineer
engineer.desc: Rigorous and technical. Prefers precision, evidence, and code-first answers.

creative.name: Creative partner
creative.desc: Exploratory and vivid. Good for writing, brainstorming, and rich examples.

companion.name: Friendly companion
companion.desc: Warm and conversational. Speaks like a friend, not a knowledge base.

safety.title: Tool approvals keep you in control
safety.subtitle: By default, Hope Agent asks before running shell commands or editing files.

point1.title: Approval gate
point1.desc: Every tool call that touches your system waits for your 'Approve' click.

point2.title: Adjustable later
point2.desc: Change the timeout in Settings → Security, or auto-approve tool calls coming from a specific IM channel (Telegram / Slack / Discord / etc.).

safety.toggleHint: Turning this off means tools run without prompting — not recommended.
safety.dangerousHint: Dangerous Mode (skip all approvals) lives in Settings → Security and requires explicit confirmation.

skills.title: Bundled skills
skills.subtitle: These ship with the app. Uncheck any you don't want available to the AI.
skills.empty: No skills were found. Click Next to continue.

server.title: How would you like to access Hope Agent?
server.subtitle: The embedded HTTP server lets you open the same app in any browser.
server.lanHint: Binds to 0.0.0.0:8420. Enable API key for protection.
server.apiKeyHint: When on, the Web GUI URL will include ?token=... so clicks from trusted clients log in automatically.
server.restartHint: Bind address changes take effect after the next restart.

channels.title: Connect an IM channel (optional)
channels.subtitle: Chat with Hope Agent from Telegram, Slack, WeChat and more. Pick any to jump to the full configuration.
channels.hint: You can always connect channels later from Settings → Channels.

summary.title: You're all set
summary.subtitle: Here's what's configured. You can revisit any of these in Settings.
summary.webHint: Open this URL on your phone, tablet, or another computer for the full GUI experience.

nav.skipDanger: Without a provider you won't be able to chat. You can finish setup later in Settings.

exit.title: Exit setup?
exit.desc: Your progress is saved. The next launch will resume on this step.

rerun.title: Re-run first-run wizard
rerun.desc: Existing configuration is kept. The wizard will walk you through each category again on next reload.

mode.title: Pick how to run
mode.subtitle: Run Hope Agent locally on this machine, or connect to an existing Hope Agent server.
mode.localDesc: Run Hope Agent on this device. Next you'll pick a model provider and finish the local setup.
mode.remoteDesc: Already have a Hope Agent server running elsewhere? Point this app at it and skip the local setup.

diffPanel.title: File changes
diffPanel.fileTooLarge: File too large, only the first 256KB is rendered

toast.draftCreated: Auto-created draft skill: {{name}}. Click to review.
